children.title: Modbus Implementation Notes and Alarm Status
children.summary: Detailed implementation notes, register value conversions, and alarm status bit field definitions.
children.content:
Notes:
1. Machine unit number is encoded as a bit field with format RRRTTTTUUUUUUUU where R represents the room number (0 = room 1, 7 = room 8), T is the machine type number (see table below), and U is the unit number.
2. Temperature and humidity values need to be divided by 10 to convert to final reading. For example, a register value of 990 is equivalent to 99.0 °F.
3. Humidity values marked ^ will be in units according to type of humidity sensor used by machine. On multi-stage machines, the sensor reads in degrees Fahrenheit wet bulb, while on single-stage machines and HVAC the sensor reads in percent relative humidity.
4. Profile day in cycle needs to be divided by 100 to convert to number of days. For example, a register value of 1800 is equivalent to day 18.00, where fractional day 0.01 is slightly less than 15 minutes.
5. Input registers marked * are active for single-stage incubators only depending on installed options.
6. The alarm relay status marked S is the only writeable register. Use the Modbus Write Register function (06) with a non-zero value to acknowledge the alarms.
7. The pressure registers marked # are active for Guardian rooms or plenums only. These two's-complement values can be positive or negative and need to be divided by 1000 to convert to units of inches water column.
8. Inactive registers will be zero. If Hatchcom has not received data from a machine for 10 minutes, its actual readings will be set to zero and alarm relay status will have a value of 2. The alarm status registers contain a bit field representation of active alarms (bit 1 is the least significant bit in the register):

Bit Alarm status LO Alarm status HI
1 High pressure Alarm bypass
2 Low pressure Damper sensor failure
3 High temperature Novram / clock error
4 High humidity Egg turn continuity
5 Low temperature Program error
6 Low humidity Carbon dioxide sensor
7 Dry wick / exhaust fan High carbon dioxide
8 Maintenance Egg temperature
9 Fan failure Pilot / HatchSense sensor
10 Door open
11 Damper failure
12 Communications
13 Egg turn failure
14 Alarm relay
15 Sensor failure
16 Sensor communications

Note that not all alarm types are implemented for every type of machine.

Machine type Description
8 Single-stage chicken incubator
9 Single-stage chicken hatcher
10 Single-stage turkey incubator
11 Single-stage turkey hatcher
14 Encore egg storage machine
16 Multi-stage incubator
17 Multi-stage hatcher
19 Guardian or Airway controlled panel
20 CM setter
21 CM hatcher
children.section: Main Screen Interface and Layout Management/Modbus TCP/SCADA Integration/Modbus Implementation Notes and Alarm Status

children.title: Database Management and Configuration
children.summary: Database backup scheduling, integrity checking, settings import/export, and archive management for Hatchcom data storage.

children.title: Database Management Functions and Operations
children.summary: Complete database management functions including backup scheduling, integrity checking, and settings management.
children.content:
The database configuration screen allows you to perform several database related tasks:

- Define interval schedule for making complete database backup.
- Schedule an immediate database backup.
- Export all critical database settings to a text file which later can be restored.
- Load an archived database backup file for generation of reports and graphs.
- See date range of numeric data contained in the database or archived backup.

If you need to restore a previously backed up database file run the Hatchcom 5 installer program, then choose the Restore database file from backup when prompted to select the components to be installed. The installer also offers the option to Restore empty database file but choosing this option will result in the loss of all data including the hatchery layout. The layout and other information may then be imported from a settings backup file.

Turn on the Adjust report data to original time zone check box to display all times exactly as recorded by the Hatchcom server in reports and graphs, otherwise if an archive is viewed in a different time zone the dates and times will be adjusted accordingly.
children.section: Main Screen Interface and Layout Management/Database Management and Configuration/Database Management Functions and Operations

children.title: Database Configuration Interface and Controls
children.summary: Complete interface for database configuration including backup scheduling, location settings, and import/export functions.
children.content:
Display the SQL Server configuration manager from Windows control panel. This may be used to configure database server to use a specific TCP port.
Check the database integrity. This operation may take several minutes to finish depending on the database size.
Display the database server's error log. Use this function when the database does not start.
When selected enables the automatic database backup function. The Hatchcom server will schedule the backup around midnight at the selected frequency. Backups will be stored in the configured location.
Select the number of days between full database backups.
Select the number of database backups to keep. When Hatchcom has reached this number of backups, it will delete the oldest file before creating the next backup.
Immediately instructs the Hatchcom server to create a backup file. Depending on database size this may take a while to complete. Hatchcom will pop up a message when backup is finished.
Choose the location where backups will be stored. If you change the location, then previous backups will be left in the previous location and do not count towards the number of backup files that are stored. Note: You must choose a location which SQL Server database engine running with limited user rights can access.
Export database settings to text file. Do not edit this file as it may fail to import later. Hatchcom will automatically export settings whenever hatchery layout changes are saved. Note: Only settings are saved, not sensor readings and related data.
Import database settings that were previously exported or automatically generated by Hatchcom when the hatchery layout was saved.
Open an archived database backup. You will be prompted for the filename, and a new Hatchcom instance will be launched. The backup restoration may take a while to process before the main screen opens. Note: Only a limited number of functions are available.
Exit the database configuration screen. All changes will be saved.
children.section: Main Screen Interface and Layout Management/Database Management and Configuration/Database Configuration Interface and Controls

children.title: Alarm Callout and Notification Systems
children.summary: Configuration of automatic phone and email alarm notifications including scheduling, contact management, alarm filtering, and email server setup.

children.title: Alarm Callout System Overview
children.summary: Complete overview of alarm callout system including modem requirements, email functionality, and notification procedures.
children.content:
Alarm callout is a feature of Hatchcom which automatically places telephone calls or sends e-mails to a list of contacts when an alarm occurs.

You can specify:
Alarms which trigger Hatchcom to initiate a call.
Time period during the day when Hatchcom can call.
Delays before Hatchcom calls or when it calls again if problem persists.

If calling by phone, it is assumed that the modem is configured and hooked up to the computer and phone jack. Refer to Windows documentation for installing and setting up the modem. If only using the e-mail functionality then no modem needs to be connected to the PC.

The Hatchcom program uses the Windows TAPI interface; therefore the modem can be shared with other TAPI compliant programs. Note however that Hatchcom cannot call out while another program is using the modem, nor if a non TAPI compliant program is active and has exclusive control of the modem.

When using a standard data modem, if the modem calls out and reaches one of the persons on the phone list, that person will get a silent connection. It is up to the person being called to conclude that Hatchcom is trying to inform the person that an alarm has occurred at the hatchery.

Using the e-mail feature requires that the computer has a permanent internet connection to access a reliable e-mail server. Once Hatchcom has forwarded the e-mail message to the mail server, it has no control over the time required to send the e-mails to their destination. It is up to the user to ensure that a reliable e-mail server is used, and all message recipients have configured their inbox to accept e-mail from the server. Some cell phone providers allow e-mails to be sent directly to the phone as text messages, which may be used to provide detailed alarm data to the contact.

When sending an e-mail, the machine with the highest priority alarm that triggered the callout will be listed first, followed by all other machines that are also alarming. Note that only the highest priority alarm will be listed for each machine, therefore each machine may have additional alarms active at the same time. Machines will be shown with their short machine identifier.

The alarm callout screen shows five property pages. The first page is for general callout settings, the second page sets days and times callout is active, the third lists the contact information, the fourth selects which alarms will generate a callout, and the last page configures the optional e-mail server settings.
children.section: Main Screen Interface and Layout Management/Alarm Callout and Notification Systems/Alarm Callout System Overview

children.title: Callout Options and Modem Configuration
children.summary: General callout options including modem selection, delay times, retry settings, and bypass switch handling.
children.content:
On this page, set the general callout options such as modem device and delay times.

When this button is turned on, Hatchcom will call out if an alarm occurs, otherwise no calls or e-mails will be processed.
This button selects whether Hatchcom will call out when a machine has an alarm condition that matches the list, but the alarm bypass switch is activated on the machine. When the button is turned on, Hatchcom will call even when bypass switch is on, otherwise Hatchcom will call only when alarm is audible (not bypassed).
Note: PT-100 machines do not have access to the alarm bypass switch state and will always call out the alarm.
This number indicates how many times Hatchcom will go through the list of phone numbers if no one answers the phone. If Hatchcom has attempted to call everyone this number of times, then Hatchcom will wait for the override time before trying again. The maximum number of times to call each person is 10.
When an alarm is detected, Hatchcom will wait for the specified number of minutes before starting the callout. If the alarm goes away during the delay time, callout will not be initiated. The minimum value for this field is 1 minute and the maximum is 90 minutes.
This defines the number of seconds Hatchcom rings the phone before it determines that there is no answer. Hatchcom will go on to the next person in the list and try again. The maximum timeout is 60. On some modems, the timeout must be set in its modem properties dialog.
This is the override delay time in minutes before callout is restarted when the alarm condition is still active. In each round of calls Hatchcom will dial each active number in the contacts list. It is not possible for Hatchcom to determine whether a call was answered. This field can be set to a maximum of 240 minutes.
The modem field allows the user to pick the modem to use for callout. The modem and driver must be configured in Windows before it can be used by Hatchcom. The modem selection can only be changed at the main Hatchcom computer.
Accept the new callout settings.
Cancel callout dialog without making changes.
children.section: Main Screen Interface and Layout Management/Alarm Callout and Notification Systems/Callout Options and Modem Configuration

children.title: Callout Time Scheduling and Restrictions
children.summary: Configuration of callout time ranges including daily schedules, weekend settings, and time restrictions.
children.content:
On this page set the day and time ranges when Hatchcom can call out.

Hatchcom allows the user to set one to three independent time ranges during which Hatchcom will perform a callout. To program the ranges, select one of the range buttons on the left. Then set one of the range type buttons at the top to select whether the range is disabled, whether it is enabled all day on the days selected below, or if it is enabled on the selected days but only after and before a certain time. At least one of the ranges must be to Days or Day and Time before Hatchcom will callout.

For example: Range 1 could be set to be active Monday to Friday, after 5:00 pm and before 6:00 am. Range 2 could be set to Saturday and Sunday without any time restrictions. Range 3 would be left disabled. With this setup Hatchcom will call out at any time except from 6:00 am to 5:00 pm Mondays to Fridays.

Select the first date range for programming.
Select the second date range for programming.
Select the third date range for programming.
Disables the selected date range. This will not affect the other date ranges which may still be set to call out.
Sets the date range to callout on the selected days of the week without any time restrictions. Click the days below to select the days when callout will be permitted.
Sets the date range to callout selected days of the week with selected time restrictions. Click the days to turn on or off and set the before and after times to choose when callout will be permitted.
When date range is set to Day and Time mode then callout will be permitted only after this time.
When date range is set to Day and Time mode then callout will be permitted only before this time.
Optionally turn on this button to schedule a daily test callout at specified time. Select a time that lies within the callout time range, the test will not occur if set to a time outside one of the active ranges.
Accept the new callout settings.
Cancel callout dialog without making changes.
children.section: Main Screen Interface and Layout Management/Alarm Callout and Notification Systems/Callout Time Scheduling and Restrictions

children.title: Contact Management and Notification Settings
children.summary: Contact list configuration including phone numbers, email addresses, scheduling preferences, and dialing options.
children.content:
On this page set the phone number or e-mail contact information for up to ten people. To set the phone number and email contact for one person, you must use two lines.

When Hatchcom detects an alarm, an e-mail notification will be sent to all entries marked Email. If the e-mail could not be transmitted to server or Dial on Fail setting is not checked, then the person at first phone entry will be called first, and the person at the bottom will be called last.

The name field is used when sending e-mail only. The phone number or e-mail fields must be filled with appropriate information, it cannot be left blank. The toggle button to the left of the name enables Hatchcom to e-mail or call the person. To temporarily remove a person from the callout list, for example due to vacation, all you need to do is turn off the button. You may also select if person is called on even or odd weeks only.

Modems allow special codes to be inserted into the phone number. For example, a comma (,) will insert a two second delay during dialing which may be required on some phone systems when connecting to an outside line. Refer to the modem's manual for more information.

Turn the contact on or off for callout. This button must be turned on for each contact otherwise it will be skipped during the callout process.
Turn this option on when contact is an e-mail address. E-mail server must be configured to use this option.
Turn this option on when contact is a phone number. Modem must be configured to use this option.
Select if contact is always called, or on odd or even weeks only. Press button to cycle through options. The week number used is in accordance with ISO 8601 standard.
Accept the new callout settings.
Cancel callout dialog without making changes.
children.section: Main Screen Interface and Layout Management/Alarm Callout and Notification Systems/Contact Management and Notification Settings

children.title: Alarm Filtering and Callout Triggers
children.summary: Configuration of which specific alarms trigger callout notifications including machine-specific and HVAC panel alarms.
children.content:
On this page configure which alarms will trigger a callout.

The callout alarm list shows all alarms that can occur. Some alarms are specific to multi-stage machines, some are specific to single-stage machines, and others are used by the Guardian system only. Only alarms that have a colored background will call out if that type of alarm occurs on any machine. If the alarm has a white background, then it will not call out.

Change all alarms to selected state.
Change all alarms to unselected state.
Hatchcom makes alarm callouts for all HVAC panels that have callout enabled in their alarm status screen.
Hatchcom disables alarm callouts for all HVAC panels, ignoring the alarm status setting.
Accept the new callout settings.
Cancel callout dialog without making changes.
children.section: Main Screen Interface and Layout Management/Alarm Callout and Notification Systems/Alarm Filtering and Callout Triggers

children.title: Email Server Configuration and Settings
children.summary: Complete email server configuration including SMTP settings, authentication, encryption, and Gmail-specific requirements.
children.content:
On this page, set the e-mail server settings when using this feature.

SMTP Server: Specify the internet address of the SMTP mail server. This could be a local company mail server or one of several free Internet-based mailing services, such as Google Mail (e.g. smtp.gmail.com).
Username: The username of a mail account on the server. Some servers require just the username, others need the full username and domain name with @ symbol in between (e.g. user@gmail.com). If your smtp server requires the username only for login but needs the domain name in the from address, then add a space character before the @ symbol.
Password: The password needed to authenticate the user on the mail server.
Server port: Most non-encrypted connections will use port 25, and servers requiring encrypted connections typically use ports 465 or 587 (e.g. Gmail supports SSL encrypted port 465 or STARTTLS command on port 587). Refer to the Internet service provider documentation to determine the correct port number. Some Internet providers may block certain ports or use non-standard port numbers.

When using Gmail and you are using 2-step verification you will need to generate an app password for Hatchcom in the Google account sign-in & security settings. When 2-step verification is not used you will need to turn on the setting allowing less secure apps.

The authenticate option must be checked for most mail servers, however some local servers may operate without authentication.
The encrypt option must be checked for most Internet based mail servers, and usually is not needed for local servers.
Use the Start TLS setting when the mail server begins with an insecure connection and upgrades it to a secure type. Typically, these servers will use port 25 or 587.
When dial on fail option is checked, the modem calls out only if Hatchcom failed to transmit the e-mail messages to the server, otherwise Hatchcom will send both e-mail and phone calls. Note that successful transfer of the message to the mail server does not guarantee that the server forwards the message in a timely manner to the recipients; Hatchcom has no way to detect whether a message has failed to be delivered. After configuring server settings and adding e-mail addresses to the contacts page, use the Test button to send out a test message. The test function is only available at the main Hatchcom computer. There will be an error message indicating the reason if Hatchcom fails to connect to the mail server. If the test message is not received by a recipient, check whether it ended up in the spam mailbox. Create a rule in the user's e-mail system to make sure future messages are received correctly.
Accept the new callout settings.
Cancel callout dialog without making changes.
children.section: Main Screen Interface and Layout Management/Alarm Callout and Notification Systems/Email Server Configuration and Settings

children.title: User Preferences and System Configuration
children.summary: Complete user preference configuration including measurement units, date formats, language settings, password security, and remote connection options.

children.title: Measurement Units Configuration
children.summary: Configuration of temperature, humidity, CO2, pressure, and decimal display units for all Hatchcom readings.
children.content:
The preferences dialog allows you to select the units used to display sensor readings such as temperature, humidity, or carbon dioxide and other user settings. The preference screen is divided into several tab pages.

Use this page to select units of measure used in Hatchcom.

Set temperature units to degrees Fahrenheit or degrees Celsius.
Set humidity units to percent relative humidity, degrees wet bulb, or use the sensor's units (relative for single-stage machines or wet bulb for multi-stage machines).
Set carbon dioxide units to percent or parts per million.
Set pressure units to metric such as Pascal and bar, or imperial such as inches of water column and pounds per square inch.
Set the decimal place mode to Windows system units, or display all decimal readings using a period or comma. Select the system decimal option to save reports using Unicode text encoding.
Display the Hatchcom software version information. See information section for details.
Accept the new preference settings.
Cancel preference dialog without making changes.
children.section: Main Screen Interface and Layout Management/User Preferences and System Configuration/Measurement Units Configuration

children.title: Date and Time Format Configuration
children.summary: Configuration of date display formats, profile day formatting, and calendar date order preferences.
children.content:
Use this page to select how dates are formatted in Hatchcom.

Select profile day in cycle to be displayed as:
2:12h fractional days
60h days and hours
12/31/99

Select calendar date order to be displayed as:
31/12/99 month/day/year
day/month/year
99/12/31 or year/month/day

Select calendar date order to be displayed using Windows system date order.
Display the Hatchcom software version information. See information section for details.
Accept the new preference settings.
Cancel preference dialog without making changes.
children.section: Main Screen Interface and Layout Management/User Preferences and System Configuration/Date and Time Format Configuration

children.title: General Preferences and Interface Options
children.summary: General preference settings including language selection, sequential addressing, embedded screens, and interface modes.
children.content:
Use this page to select various preference options.

Select language to display in Hatchcom. A dialog will list all languages that are installed. Select the desired language or choose the blank option to hide text on all function buttons.
Sequential addressing mode allows newer Platinum machines to use a continuous address numbering system. Hatchcom will remove room numbers unless a room assignment was made in layout properties. Consult the Platinum user manual for the procedure on setting the address switches to work with sequential addressing mode.
Embedded main screen option allows Hatchcom to show an embedded state screen in the layout when sufficiently zoomed in. Note that this option greatly increases the memory usage of the Hatchcom program.
Change the password configuration.
Select the light user interface mode. The Hatchcom client will restart when mode is changed.
Select the dark user interface mode. The Hatchcom client will restart when mode is changed.
On Windows systems that support dark mode, adapts Hatchcom to use the Windows default app mode. Hatchcom will use light user interface mode if Windows does not support the feature.
Display the Hatchcom software version information. See information section for details.
Accept the new preference settings.
Cancel preference dialog without making changes.

The Do not use native file selection dialogs option only needs to be enabled in rare instances where a faulty Windows Explorer shell extension interferes with Hatchcom operation when selecting file names, for example when saving a report.
The Use classic print dialog option uses the legacy print dialog on Windows 11 where the embedded preview function is not available in Hatchcom. Note that this option will affect all Windows applications. Select the square option to restore the Windows default mode.
The Sound alarms on computer option can be enabled to play a sound on the local PC whenever there is an audible incubator alarm.
children.section: Main Screen Interface and Layout Management/User Preferences and System Configuration/General Preferences and Interface Options

children.title: Language Selection and Display Options
children.summary: Language selection for interface text including language-neutral icon-based navigation.
children.content:
Use the language setting to select the language to be used for text items in the Hatchcom program. The dialog will list all the languages currently installed. You may also select a language neutral option that suppresses most of the English text, allowing navigation by icons.

Select the desired language as indicated by the name underneath the icon. The button with a blank name will select language neutral setting which removes all English text from command buttons.
Accept the new language setting.
Cancel language dialog without making changes.
children.section: Main Screen Interface and Layout Management/User Preferences and System Configuration/Language Selection and Display Options

children.title: Password Security Configuration
children.summary: Complete password security configuration including level 1 and 2 passwords, authentication modes, and protected functions.
children.content:
Hatchcom provides password security access to functions that modify how the incubators and hatchers operate. Functions such as reports and graphs are not password protected since they do not affect machine operation.

When Hatchcom is connected to a Platinum or Sentry Network, Hatchcom will use the same passwords.

Two levels of access are provided:
- Use Level 1 Password (lower) for hatchery personnel who can change a few of the machine parameters.
- Use Level 2 Password (higher) to give your hatchery manager access to all machine functions.

Each of the two levels is programmed with its own four-digit numeric password.

Edit password level 1 value. When prompted enter password value between 1 and 9999. To disable the password press C to clear the value.
Edit password level 2 value. When prompted enter password value between 1 and 9999. To disable the password press C to clear the value.
No Password - The selected function does not require a password to enter.
Level 1 - Use a level 1 password (lower) for hatchery personnel who can change a few of the machine's parameters. Any function that requires a level 1 password can also be accessed using the level 2 password.
Level 2 - Use level 2 password (higher) to give your hatchery manager access to all machine functions but restrict access to all other employees.
Level 2 Master - The function requires the level 2 password and can only be entered at the main Hatchcom console or local Platinum display.
Master display - The function does not require a password, but users can only enter the function on the main Hatchcom console, local Platinum display, or master Sentry display.
When selected, Hatchcom will prompt for password every time a function is accessed that requires a password.
When selected Hatchcom will prompt for password one time only the first time a protected function is accessed. You will not be asked again until program is closed.
When selected Hatchcom will never prompt for password. Note: The user will still be prompted for a password at Platinum touch screens and Sentry display panels.
When selected Hatchcom uses Windows logon name to authenticate users. When selecting this option, you will be prompted to set the password users and assign the level for each user. Unlisted users will be prompted to enter the password, but if the special * name is added then this level is used instead without prompting for password.
Accept the new password settings.
Cancel password dialog without making changes.
children.section: Main Screen Interface and Layout Management/User Preferences and System Configuration/Password Security Configuration

children.title: Password-Protected Function List
children.summary: Complete list of Hatchcom functions that can be password protected with their respective access levels.
children.content:
Note: If you are setting a level 1 password and there is currently no level 2 password, level 1 and 2 passwords will be set to the same password. If the level 2 password is disabled, then the level 1 password is also disabled. It is acceptable to have a level 2 password and not have a password for level 1.

The following table shows the functions that can be password protected. Some functions will affect several related operations.

1 Start profile, adjust profile start date, transfer profile start date, stage control
2 Edit profile, edit current profile, delete profile, edit stage program
3 Copy profile
4 Change password
5 Select online/offline status
6 Configure single-stage, multi-stage, or Guardian HVAC network
7 Hatchcom options: alarm callout, database configuration, edit maintenance task, configure recurring maintenance tasks
8 Adjust clock (at machine only)
9 Graph sample time (at machine only)
10 Dry out mode
11 Reset machine (at machine only)
children.section: Main Screen Interface and Layout Management/User Preferences and System Configuration/Password-Protected Function List

children.title: Password User Management and Authentication
children.summary: Windows user authentication configuration and access level assignment for Hatchcom users.
children.content:
The password user setup screen is shown when the user authentication mode is selected. Use the screen to enter the Windows logon identifiers of all Hatchcom users, assigning the password level they have access to. The listed users will not be asked to enter passwords anymore when working from a Hatchcom client. Optionally the special name * may be added to the list to represent users that are not explicitly listed. This could be used to block access to functions that change machine operation, however users would still be able to see the machine status, reports and graphs.

Add the specified login identifier to the list. Do not include the domain name. Click the authentication level buttons on the left to assign the access permission.
Remove the selected login identifier from the list.
When selected, the user will be blocked from accessing all critical machine functions.
When selected, the user will only be able to access functions that do not require a password.
When selected, the user will only be able to access functions that require level 1 access or that do not require a password.
When selected, the user will have access to all critical machine functions. If a function requires master access, then the user will only be able to perform the function at the main Hatchcom console.
Exit the password user list dialog.
children.section: Main Screen Interface and Layout Management/User Preferences and System Configuration/Password User Management and Authentication

children.title: Remote Connection Configuration
children.summary: Configuration of remote client connections including server selection, security phrases, and firewall considerations.
children.content:
Use this page to select remote connection options.

Remote mode enables the Hatchcom client to connect to the networked server on a different computer. Remote connection may fail if firewalls are blocking access.
When remote mode is turned on, choose the server computer name from a list of local PCs. Alternatively, you may enter the computer name or IP address directly into the entry field.
Use an optional security phrase to authenticate remote clients. When the phrase is set at the main Hatchcom PC, enter the matching phrase at each remote client else the connection is rejected.
Display the Hatchcom software version information. See information section for details.
Accept the new preference settings.
Cancel preference dialog without making changes.
children.section: Main Screen Interface and Layout Management/User Preferences and System Configuration/Remote Connection Configuration

children.title: 360 Cloud Platform Connection Configuration
children.summary: Configuration of 360 cloud platform connections including edge server setup, token management, and network addressing.
children.content:
Use this page to select 360 cloud platform connection options. After entering the user and network connection options press the 'Request token' button to establish communication with the edge server device. If connection is successful the security key will appear beside the padlock icon, otherwise the message area shows the failure reason.

The 360 platform data transfer can be disabled by pressing the 'Reset token' button.

Select the edge server user. The user must be set to 'hatchcom'.
Select the edge server password. The password must be set to the name of the edge server displayed on its nameplate label, for example 'Edge-1a1234'.
The network address of the edge server. The server will obtain a DHCP lease from local network and register the DNS name displayed on the nameplate label, for example 'Edge-1a1234'. If the search function fails to locate the server and the network is not resolving the name to the IP address, then the network administrator needs to examine DHCP server for its address. In this case the address should be reserved so that it does not change over time. Alternatively, the secondary Ethernet port of the edge server may be connected to the private Platinum incubator Ethernet network. The Hatchcom PC will be able to access the edge server via its fixed '10.77.254.254' static IP address.
Search the local network for edge server located on the same /24 subnet as the Hatchcom PC. A specific subnet can be searched by entering an IPv4 address into the network address field. When an edge server is discovered, its local DNS name is filled into the network address field, else its IP address when the name could not be resolved.
Shows the edge server token when Hatchcom has connected to the server.
Shows the status message from the edge server. When Hatchcom is connected to the edge server it will show the time of last data transfer.
Display the Hatchcom software version information. See information section for details.
Accept the new preference settings.
Cancel preference dialog without making changes.
children.section: Main Screen Interface and Layout Management/User Preferences and System Configuration/360 Cloud Platform Connection Configuration

title: Report Generation and Data Analysis
summary: Comprehensive reporting system for hatchery equipment monitoring including screen reports, alarm history, statistical analysis, numeric data, setpoint tracking, profile changes, and maintenance scheduling.

children.title: Report System Overview and Navigation
children.summary: Common interface elements, navigation controls, and data selection options for all Hatchcom report types.

children.title: Report Types and Functionality Overview
children.summary: Complete overview of all available report types including screen reports, alarm reports, statistics, numeric data, setpoint tracking, profiles, and maintenance.
children.content:
Hatchcom provides several different types of reports that allow you to obtain detailed information about the current execution of the machines or past machine activity. The following reports are available:

- Screen report lists the current setpoints and actual readings of all machines.
- Alarm report lists past alarm activity showing date and time when alarm started and ended, along with sensor readings at the time alarm commenced.
- Statistic report displays averaged sensor readings over a time period, along with the minimum and maximum readings and activity percentage of heating, cooling and other outputs.
- Numeric report presents a detailed listing of temperature, humidity, carbon dioxide and other readings for selected machine over a time period.
- Setpoint report lists all setpoint changes made by a machine over a time period.
- Profile report lists all profile and setting changes made by operator over a time period.
- Scheduled maintenance report allows you to set periodic maintenance tasks for incubators and HVAC units and keep track of tasks that have been completed.

All reports have a similar screen layout with many options to select the type of machines and data columns to be included.

The statistic report screenshot below shows the common report features. Note that not all reports have the same feature set and some items are displayed differently.

- Room and machine type selections are only available in global reports selected from the ribbon of the Home screen.
- Data columns may be turned on or off from the Columns menu in some reports or have dedicated selection buttons instead of the menu.
- Reports displaying historical data have start and end controls to select the date range. All dates will be formatted as selected by date selection in preference screen.
- The alarm, numeric and setpoint reports present the data in a text format that can be selected and copied to another program such as Microsoft Excel.
The screen, profile and statistic report have context menus to obtain detailed information on selected unit.
children.section: Report Generation and Data Analysis/Report System Overview and Navigation/Report Types and Functionality Overview

children.title: Common Report Controls and Functions
children.summary: Complete set of common control buttons and functions available across all report types.
children.content:
Save report to disk file in tab delimited format or comma separated values format. The data can then be imported into other programs such as Microsoft Excel.
Preview the report for printing. You will be able to change options before printing the report to any Windows compatible printer device.
Print the report to any Windows compatible printer device.
Open a menu to select the data columns to be included in the report. Click on each menu item to turn on and off. The menu item will have a blue background when selected, or white when not selected.
Hide all columns that do not contain any data.
Show all columns including ones that are presently turned off.
On reports that display historical data set the start date and time. If this button cannot be pressed, change the date or time entry field beside it.
On reports that display historical data set the end date and time. If this button cannot be pressed, change the date or time entry field beside it.
On reports that display historical data, set the date range to cover the last 24 hours. The report will refresh immediately when pressed.
Refresh button appears when the user changes a date or time setting. Click the refresh button to reload the report data using selected date range.
Show single-stage incubators in report when selected. Hold button down to clear or select all machine types.
Show single-stage hatchers in report when selected. Hold button down to clear or select all machine types.
Show Platinum egg storage machines in report when selected. Hold button down to clear or select all machine types.
Show multi-stage incubators in report when selected. Hold button down to clear or select all machine types.
Show multi-stage hatchers in report when selected. Hold button down to clear or select all machine types.
Show CM setters in report when selected. Hold button down to clear or select all machine types.
Show CM hatchers in report when selected. Hold button down to clear or select all machine types.
Show Guardian HVAC panels in report when selected. Hold button down to clear or select all machine types.
Show Sentry display panels in report when selected. Hold button down to clear or select all machine types.
Show units assigned to room when selected. Any units that do not use room will be shown when room 1 is selected. Hold button down to clear or select all rooms.
Load report for previous machine.
Load report for next machine.
In the screen and statistic reports, click the right mouse button on a unit to show a context menu with additional functions. See context menu in the main screen for more information. In numeric, alarm, and setpoint reports the right mouse button will allow you to copy selected text and paste into another program such as Microsoft Excel.
children.section: Report Generation and Data Analysis/Report System Overview and Navigation/Common Report Controls and Functions

children.title: Screen Report and Real-Time Monitoring
children.summary: Real-time monitoring display showing current setpoints, actual readings, alarms, and machine status for all hatchery equipment.

children.title: Screen Report Display and Content
children.summary: Complete description of screen report content including sensor readings, alarms, status indicators, and machine-specific data.
children.content:
Use the screen report to display all current setpoint and actual readings for each incubator or Guardian-controlled room or plenum. The values are constantly updated as they change. The screen report displays the following readings:

Active alarms
Temperature setpoint and actual
Humidity setpoint and actual
Damper setpoint, actual and auto settings (single-stage only)
Carbon dioxide setpoint and actual (single-stage only)
Pressure setpoint and actual (Guardian only)
Fan speed (single-stage and Guardian only)
Auxiliary fan (CM hatchers only)
Turn count (multi-stage or single-stage using compatible software)
Profile number, start date and elapsed incubation time (incubators and hatchers only)
Calibration offsets (single-stage and PS501 controllers only)

The alarm column lists the currently active alarms. Hover the mouse over the icons to see a tooltip with the alarm descriptions.

Setpoints and readings will be displayed using units set in the preference screen, with the exception that the Guardian system will always show humidity in percent relative humidity. Readings will be color coded based on sensor type when enabled in Columns option menu.

Some of the columns may be blank depending on the machine type and whether the unit is equipped with the required sensor. For example, HVAC plenums do not have temperature or humidity sensors, therefore those columns will appear empty.

Dual zone Platinum incubators will show two temperature actual readings and temperature calibration offsets. The first value indicates the front zone, and the second value the rear zone.

CM machines show the cooling mode in the temperature setpoint column, the damper mode in the damper setpoint column, and blower mode in the auxiliary fan column. See setpoints section for explanation of the mode icons. The profile column shows the program step number.

The column widths can be adjusted by dragging the separator between the column headers. Click on any header item to sort the values according to the values in that column.

The button controls are described in report navigation section.
children.section: Report Generation and Data Analysis/Screen Report and Real-Time Monitoring/Screen Report Display and Content

children.title: Alarm History and Event Reporting
children.summary: Detailed alarm history reporting with start/end times, acknowledgment tracking, sensor readings, and alarm analysis.

children.title: Alarm Report Functions and Access Methods
children.summary: Multiple access methods for alarm reports and complete description of alarm data storage and display.
children.content:
The alarm report function provides a detailed summary of alarms that have occurred. The alarms can be shown for a specific machine of interest, or a report of all machines of a given type can be generated depending on where you select the alarm report function:

- Main screen toolbar: display alarm report for all machines.
- Group selection function: display alarm report for selected machines only.
- Context menu: display alarm report for one specific machine only.
- Alarm status windows: display alarm report for one specific machine only.

Hatchcom will store up to 100,000 alarm occurrences, after which the oldest alarms will be erased from the database.

The button controls are described in report navigation section, with the following additional functions:
A progress bar is shown while data is loading. The information is presented in several columns.
- First column shows the unit's address in short or long form depending on the selection in Columns menu. This column will not appear when a report is for a single machine.
- Second column indicates the alarm type.
- Third column indicates the zone number if the machine has more than one zone and the alarm type can be indicated separately for each zone. More than one number is shown if alarm was active on multiple zones.
- Fourth column indicates the start date and time of the alarm.
- Fifth column indicates the end date and time of the alarm. If the alarm is still active, then the end time area will be blank since the alarm has not ended yet.
- Sixth and seventh columns indicate the last time the alarm was acknowledged and how many times it was acknowledged. If the alarm was never acknowledged, the time will be blank.
- Eighth column indicates the duration of the alarm If there is a start and end time. The units will be seconds, minutes, hours, or days depending on the duration of the alarm.
- Ninth and tenth columns show the profile and incubation day in cycle at the time of alarm, shown as per date preference. These will be blank if profile was not active. The asterisk marker * indicates the profile is a Pilot type on incubators, HatchSense on hatchers, Encore type on egg storage machines, or schedule on HVAC. On CM machines the eighth column shows the program step number.
- Remaining columns show the setpoint and actual readings at the time when the alarm first occurred. Some readings will be blank depending on machine capabilities.

See alarm description section for detailed explanation of the alarms. If you turn off the long unit description option in the columns menu then machines will be shown with its short machine identifier.
children.section: Report Generation and Data Analysis/Alarm History and Event Reporting/Alarm Report Functions and Access Methods

children.title: Statistical Analysis and Performance Reporting
children.summary: Statistical analysis of machine performance including averages, minimums/maximums, standard deviation, and output activity percentages.

children.title: Statistic Report Functions and Calculations
children.summary: Statistical calculations including averages, minimums, maximums, and output activity percentages with 24-hour performance evaluation.
children.content:
The statistic report function uses a machine's historical data to calculate averages, minimums, and maximums for temperature, humidity, carbon dioxide and other sensor readings. It also calculates the activity percentage of outputs such as heating, cooling, humidification and damper.

The activity percentage is a calculation of the amount of time an output was active during the previous recording period. The Hatchcom server records sensor readings every five minutes, therefore if the heating percentage shows 40%, then heating was on for a total of 2 minutes over the previous 5-minute recording period.

Press the 24-hour button to quickly evaluate machine performance for the last day. Hatchcom will set the date range to cover the preceding 24 hours and reload the statistic report using the new date range.

The column widths can be adjusted by dragging the separator between the column headers. Click on any header item to sort the values according to the values in that column. Hover the mouse over a header item to see a tooltip showing the full description.

Setpoints and readings will be displayed using units set in the preference screen, with the exception that Guardian-controlled rooms will always show humidity in percent relative humidity. Readings will be color coded based on sensor type when enabled in Columns menu under options setting:

Red for temperature sensor.
Blue for humidity sensor.
Orange for damper sensor.
Green for carbon dioxide sensor.
Yellow for pressure sensor.

Generates detailed statistics for selected unit showing daily averages and standard deviation over the current date range.
children.section: Report Generation and Data Analysis/Statistical Analysis and Performance Reporting/Statistic Report Functions and Calculations

children.title: Detailed Statistical Analysis and Calculations
children.summary: Advanced statistical analysis including standard deviation, normal distribution, setpoint differences, and HVAC-specific calculations.
children.content:
The statistical report function uses a machine's historical data to calculate statistical figures for temperature, humidity, carbon dioxide and pressure readings. The computer will perform the calculations and after a short delay the window with the results is shown. The calculations will be slightly different for Guardian HVAC system and incubators.

The standard statistical analysis is made on the actual readings. The average and standard deviation of the actual readings are calculated, and the maximum and minimum reading that occurred each day during the selected time period. Dual zone incubators will show two temperature readings, the first for front zone and the second for rear zone.

Incubators and hatchers have the option to calculate statistical analysis on the difference between the setpoint and actual reading. When using profiles, it can be more meaningful to calculate an average temperature that is not affected by profile setpoint changes. When the option is enabled in the Columns menu the average and standard deviation of the difference is calculated, and the maximum and minimum difference that occurred each day during the selected time period.

On incubators equipped with a carbon dioxide sensor, the average actual carbon dioxide is displayed, as well as the maximum and minimum. On Guardian HVAC panels equipped with pressure sensor, the report will list average, maximum, and minimum pressure. Note that unlike temperature and humidity, these calculations never consider the setpoint value.

Next to the statistical information there will be additional columns showing heating, cooling, humidification, dehumidification, and damper activity information. The numbers show what percentage of time the various devices were turned on.

This manual will not go into detail of the meaning of the statistical numbers, only a brief introduction is given. The average is simply the sum of all actual readings (or differences depending on option) divided by the number of sample points.

The standard deviation is a measure of the spread of the actual readings (or differences). It assumes that the data follows what is called a normal distribution, which is a bell-shaped curve, with the average representing the middle of the curve. It shows how far away from the average point the readings are expected to be. A sample curve is shown in the figure. The first standard deviation is 34.1%, the second is 47.7% and the third is 49.9%. In a normal distribution it is always expected that half of the readings would be above the average and the other half below the average.

As an example, a statistical report of an incubator using the differences option indicates that the average temperature difference is -0.1 F and the standard deviation is 0.15 F as shown in the figure. Ideally the average difference would be close to zero which would indicate that the actual reading was the same as the setpoint or was above the setpoint about as often as it was below the setpoint. If the average is negative, then the actual readings tended to be below the setpoint. In this example 34.1% of all differences of the sampled points would be below the average difference by no more than 0.15 F (between -0.25 and -0.1 on the sample curve), that 47.7% of all differences would be below the average by no more than 0.3°F between -0.4 and -0.1 and that 49.9% of the differences would be below the average difference by no more than 0.45°F between -0.55 and -0.10. This same reasoning can be used for differences above the average difference. Note: The above assumes that the differences form a normal distribution. For this example, 81.8% of all temperature readings (34.1 + 47.7) would be within -0.25 and +0.2°F of the setpoint.

The button controls are described in report navigation section.
children.section: Report Generation and Data Analysis/Statistical Analysis and Performance Reporting/Detailed Statistical Analysis and Calculations

children.title: Numeric Data Reporting and Export
children.summary: Detailed numerical data reporting with configurable sampling intervals, data export capabilities, and comprehensive sensor reading documentation.

children.title: Numeric Report Overview and Data Storage
children.summary: Overview of numeric report functionality including data storage duration, activity percentage calculations, and export options.
children.content:
The numeric report function creates a detailed listing of temperature, humidity and damper setpoint readings recorded by Hatchcom. The activity percentage of heating, cooling, humidification, and damper activity can also be displayed. This is the same data that is used by the line graph function, but here the data can be inspected in numerical form.

Numeric data is stored in the database for 180 days, after which the oldest data will be erased.

The activity percentage is a calculation of the amount of time an output was active during the previous recording period. The Hatchcom server records sensor readings every five minutes, therefore if the heating percentage shows 40%, then heating was on for a total of 2 minutes over the previous 5-minute recording period.

Press the 24-hour button to quickly evaluate machine performance for the last day. Hatchcom will set the date range to cover the preceding 24 hours and reload the numeric report using the new date range.

As an alternative to saving the data to disk, you may use copy and paste operation to transfer data into programs such as Microsoft Excel. Select the text by dragging the left mouse button, then press Ctrl+C or click right mouse button and choose Copy. In Microsoft Excel use Ctrl+V or the Paste function to insert the data.
children.section: Report Generation and Data Analysis/Numeric Data Reporting and Export/Numeric Report Overview and Data Storage

children.title: Numeric Report Columns and Data Structure
children.summary: Complete description of numeric report columns including time stamps, profile data, sensor readings, and activity percentages.
children.content:
The button controls are described in report navigation section, with the following additional functions:
Show the temperature and humidity calibration offsets.
Shows the profile and day in cycle values.
Use the slider to select the font point size. When button is turned on printing will use the selected point size which may result in some text cut off page, otherwise printed report will be scaled to fit the width of paper.
Use the timer interval slider to select whether all data is included in report, or data is averaged to produce time intervals of 5, 10, 15, 30, 60 or 120 minutes.
Load report for previous machine.
Load report for next machine.
A progress bar is shown while data is loading. The information is presented in several columns.
- The first section shows the date and time when the sample was taken. The sampling rate defaults to 5 minutes but can be changed with the interval slider. By default, the dates are formatted as selected by the Windows regional settings but can be overridden in the preferences.
- The second section shows the profile and incubation day in cycle. These will be blank if profile was not active. Day in cycle is formatted as per the date preference setting. The asterisk marker * indicates the profile is a Pilot type on incubators, HatchSense type on hatchers, Encore type on egg storage machines, or schedule on HVAC. On CM machines the profile column shows the program step number. When the machine is in standby mode, the day in cycle is blank. When a prewarm step is active it will show the number of hours until profile start.
- The third section shows the temperature setpoint and actual readings. The Max and Min values indicate the maximum and minimum air temperature readings during the previous sampling period. When the machine is a dual zone type, separate columns show actual temperature for each zone. Values are displayed in units as selected by the preference screen.
- If egg temperature is enabled and machine is equipped with a Pilot system, the actual eggshell temperatures will be displayed next.
- The fourth section shows the humidity setpoint and actual values, and the maximum and minimum humidity in the previous sample period. All values are displayed in units as selected by the preference screen, except for HVAC panels which will always show percent relative humidity.
- The fifth section for single-stage machines contains damper data, indicating the actual damper position, the damper setpoint and auto damper setting. The damper setpoint column will be blank if the machine is operating in carbon dioxide mode, and the auto damper column will be blank if auto damper is disabled. On CM machines equipped with anemometer the air velocity and volume will be displayed using cfm units if machine temperature mode is set to °F or m³/min if display is set to °C.
- The sixth section displays carbon dioxide setpoint and actual values in units as selected by the preference screen when equipped with a sensor. The setpoint column will be blank if machine is operating using a fixed damper setpoint.
- The seventh section for Guardian rooms displays pressure setpoint and actual readings for units configured with this option.
- The eight section shows the activity percentages of output events such as heating and cooling. The number of columns and output list depends on the machine type.
- If the HVAC panel supports the operating mode setting, then a numeric value will indicate the current mode: 0 is rooftop unit off, 1 is normal/automatic, 2 is cooling only, 3 is heating only, 4 – 6 are economy auto, cooling, and heating respectively, and 7 is fan only mode.
- Single-stage and PS501 multi-stage incubators show the rack turn feedback signal. This column shows v¹ when racks have turned left, or v¹ when racks have turned right. It will show C if racks are in process of turning, but this may not always appear in report depending on timing of samples. The status will be blank if it is not available or single-stage racks fail to turn after five minutes. Machine controllers with compatible firmware show the rack turn count. Note: When report is saved the turn indicators change to < for left turn, > for right turn, or ~ for turning state.
- Single-stage machines and PS501 multi-stage controllers using firmware version 4.20 or higher show the temperature and humidity calibration offsets. Note: Offsets are not recorded by Hatchcom versions prior to 4.11.

children.title: Setpoint Change History and Tracking
children.summary: Comprehensive tracking of all setpoint changes including operator interventions, profile executions, calibration adjustments, and schedule modifications.

children.title: Setpoint Report Overview and Data Collection
children.summary: Overview of setpoint change tracking including data storage capacity, change reasons, and calibration recording.
children.content:
Hatchcom records all setpoint changes that occur on a machine, either as a result of operator intervention, or by the machine as it executes a profile. Hatchcom records the last 500,000 setpoint changes covering all machines. This includes the time and day of the change, the new setpoints, and the reason for the change.

Hatchcom also records changes to calibration offsets of temperature and humidity sensors on single-stage machines, or PS501 multi-stage controllers that have firmware version 4.21 or higher. Single-stage Platinum machines will record carbon dioxide calibration changes when the Platinum touchscreen display uses firmware version 4.24 or higher.

The button controls are described in report navigation section, with the following additional functions:
Load report for previous machine.
Load report for next machine.
A progress bar is shown while data is loading. The information is presented in several columns.
children.section: Report Generation and Data Analysis/Setpoint Change History and Tracking/Setpoint Report Overview and Data Collection

children.title: Setpoint Report Columns and Change Types
children.summary: Complete description of setpoint report columns including change types, user identification, and calibration events.
children.content:
The first two columns show the date and time when the setpoint change occurred. The third column shows which profile number was active or became active. If no profile was active, this column shows a dash. An asterisk character * is displayed if the profile is a Pilot type on incubators, HatchSense type on hatchers, Encore type on egg storage machines, or schedule on Guardian HVAC panels. On CM machines the profile column shows the program step number. The fourth column shows the day in cycle of the profile if it was active. If the machine was in standby mode, this field will show 'Standby' or the number of hours remaining until profile start if a prewarm step is active. The next columns show the new temperature, humidity, damper, and carbon dioxide or pressure setpoints. On dual zone machines the rear temperature column will be blank because the setpoint is common to both zones. If the event is a calibration change, the temperature and humidity columns show the new calibration deviation from the factory setting. When the old calibration offset is available, the previous offset is shown to the left of y symbol, and the new offset to the right. The last two columns show what activity took place when the change occurred and the login name of the user if the change was initiated from Hatchcom. The username will be blank if the action was made at the machine. The action will show:

Setpoints changed: An operator changed the fixed setpoints from Hatchcom.
Profile started: A new profile was started. This may also appear on a machine whenever it is powered off and on while a profile is running.
Profile changed: An operator instructed the machine to use a different profile number, by starting a different profile using the same start date and time.
Profile cancelled: The machine was in profile mode, but an operator activated fixed setpoints which cancels profile mode. This may also appear on a machine whenever it is powered off and on while the machine is running a profile.
Delayed start: On CM machines program start was delayed. Another setpoint record will indicate when machine switches to stage program mode.
Sensors calibrated: This action is not a change in setpoint, it indicates that one of the sensors was calibrated. The temperature and humidity columns indicate the new offsets, or the change from old to new offset when available. Note that the humidity offset is always shown in units compatible with the humidity sensor, irrespective of the current humidity preference setting.
Return calibrated: On CM machines this action indicates that the return temperature probes were calibrated. The temperature columns indicate the change from old to new return probe offsets.
Zero calibrated: This action is not a change in setpoint. This indicates that the carbon dioxide sensor's zero point was calibrated. If an instrument was used to calibrate, then the CO2 column indicates the new calibration reading. The column will be blank when using calibration gas or show a dash when resetting the calibration.
Span calibrated: This action is not a change in setpoint. This indicates that the carbon dioxide sensor's span point was calibrated. If an instrument was used to calibrate, then the CO2 column indicates the new calibration reading. The column will be blank when using calibration gas or show a dash when resetting the calibration.
Calibration checked: This action is not a change in setpoint. This indicates that the carbon dioxide sensor calibration was checked when user used the calibration command without making a change. Platinum machines must be running software version that supports this feature.
Setpoints locked: The setpoints were locked on a multi-stage machine.
Setpoints unlocked: The setpoints were unlocked on a multi-stage machine.
Hatchcom started: The Hatchcom computer was restarted.
Blank: The machine made the setpoint change executing a profile, or the setpoints were changed by the operator at the machine. Note that the username field will also be blank because Hatchcom cannot determine who changed the setpoints.
children.section: Report Generation and Data Analysis/Setpoint Change History and Tracking/Setpoint Report Columns and Change Types
